# AI Incident Law

> Open, curated public-record corpus of legal and regulatory matters involving AI-related incidents, failures, and harms. Searchable by humans and agents. Code MIT, dataset CC BY 4.0. Stewarded by PAICE.work PBC. This is a public-record reference, not legal advice.

Each record resolves to a primary or stable secondary public source. Records carry their own `last_verified_date` / `last_checked_date`; check it before presenting a record as current. The dataset is curated for source quality over volume, not crawled.

## Data

- [Canonical dataset](https://aiincidentlaw.org/data/data.json): Source-of-truth JSON. Datasets: `included` (admitted public matters), `review` (editorial queue), `global` (non-US, pending sourcing).
- [Browser bundle](https://aiincidentlaw.org/data.js): Generated read-only bundle consumed by the site.
- [Data schema](https://aiincidentlaw.org/docs/data-schema.md): Field intent and record shape.
- [Methodology](https://aiincidentlaw.org/docs/methodology.html): Scope, admission criteria, source quality, freshness, and queue handling.

## Obligation-First API

- [API manifest](https://aiincidentlaw.org/api/v1/of/index.json): Available generated files and counts.
- [Authorities](https://aiincidentlaw.org/api/v1/of/authorities.json): Courts, tribunals, and agencies.
- [Proceedings](https://aiincidentlaw.org/api/v1/of/proceedings.json): Filed matters and actions.
- [Allegations](https://aiincidentlaw.org/api/v1/of/allegations.json): AI-related failures asserted in public records.
- [Determinations](https://aiincidentlaw.org/api/v1/of/determinations.json): Resolving postures for included matters.

## Agent discovery

- [agents.json](https://aiincidentlaw.org/agents.json): Capabilities, API endpoints, MCP actions, and legal graph layer metadata.
- [MCP registration](https://aiincidentlaw.org/.well-known/mcp.json): Public MCP and static query endpoints.
- [Obligation-First naming profile](https://aiincidentlaw.org/.well-known/obligation-first-naming-profile.jsonld): IRI scheme and identifier crosswalks for each entity type (Authority, Proceeding, Allegation, Determination).

## PAICE legal graph

AI Incident Law is layer 4 (Case & Enforcement Evidence) of the PAICE legal graph. The graph is a connected representation of AI law from schema to statute to record to consequence:

1. [Obligation-First](https://obligationfirst.org/) -- upper schema. IRI vocabulary shared across all layers. agents.json: https://obligationfirst.org/agents.json
2. [EveryAILaw](https://everyailaw.com/) -- AI obligation registry. Laws and the obligations they create. agents.json: https://everyailaw.com/agents.json / MCP: https://everyailaw.com/.well-known/mcp.json
3. [PubLedge](https://publedge.org/) -- open civic recordkeeping. Protocol and templates for transparent civic records. agents.json: https://publedge.org/agents.json / MCP: https://publedge.org/.well-known/mcp.json
4. AI Incident Law (this site) -- case & enforcement evidence. What courts and agencies actually did when obligations were breached.

Records in this dataset may carry `obligation_first_anchors`: IRIs linking a case to the specific obligation(s) from EveryAILaw that the incident implicates. This makes the graph traversable: from obligation to consequence and back. Cross-graph narrative: https://aiincidentlaw.org/docs/legal-graph.html

## About

- [Project home](https://aiincidentlaw.org/): Searchable single-page reference.
- [Methodology](https://aiincidentlaw.org/docs/methodology.html): Corpus scope, admission criteria, source policy, freshness, and exclusions.
- [How this fits](https://aiincidentlaw.org/docs/legal-graph.html): The four-layer graph explained with a cross-graph query example.
- [Submit a case](https://aiincidentlaw.org/docs/submit-a-case.html): Contribution criteria and workflow for public-record case submissions.
- [INTENT](https://github.com/snapsynapse/ai-incident-law/blob/main/INTENT.md): Stewardship principles, admission criteria, refresh cadence.

## Attribution

AI Incident Law, PAICE.work PBC, CC BY 4.0. Source project: https://aiincidentlaw.org/
